user_query:
I'm a fourth-generation descendant of Jacob Young and I need clean, verifiable minutes from our family association's recent planning call. Using only the files under input/, please write and run a local script that parses the materials and produces three outputs under output/:

1) output/meeting_summary.json — a structured JSON summary with at least these fields:
- meeting_title (string)
- meeting_date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- start_time (HH:MM, local ET) and end_time (HH:MM, local ET) if present
- attendees (array of full names matched to input/attendees.csv)
- guests (array of names present but not in input/attendees.csv)
- agenda (array of {title, duration_minutes})
- decisions (array of {summary, sources}), where sources is an array of file paths where the decision was found
- action_items (array of {task, owner, due_date, project_id, source}), where:
  • owner is a canonical name from input/attendees.csv when possible, otherwise a free-text guest name
  • due_date is standardized as YYYY-MM-DD; for dates without a year (e.g., 3/20 or 03/18), use year 2026
  • project_id is linked from input/projects.json if a literal ID like (P-00X) is present; if no ID, attempt an exact title match; otherwise null
  • source is the file path where the item was extracted
- next_meeting (object with date and time if present)
- validation (object with: total_decisions, total_action_items, matched_attendees, unmatched_attendees_count)

2) output/minutes.md — human-readable minutes with clear sections for Title, Date/Time, Attendees, Agenda, Decisions, Action Items (with owner and due date), and Next Meeting. Include a brief line indicating it was generated by your script.

3) output/run_report.txt — a short run report that includes: the exact command you ran (e.g., `python3 scripts/prepare_minutes.py`), the list of input files parsed, counts for decisions and action items extracted, counts of matched vs. unmatched attendees, and any warnings (e.g., actions missing an owner or due date).

Requirements and constraints:
- Implement the logic as a script saved under scripts/ (e.g., scripts/prepare_minutes.py) and actually run it to generate the outputs.
- Parse and merge information across all input files; deduplicate exact-duplicate action items and decisions across sources (keep one with combined sources). If content differs, keep both.
- Normalize attendee names by matching against input/attendees.csv for the attendees list. Any names seen in the notes/transcripts/chats that are not in attendees.csv should be listed under guests and counted as unmatched.
- Do not use the Internet; rely solely on the provided files.
- Use 2026 as the year for any due dates missing a year, based on the meeting date in the materials.

Input is in input/. Write code under scripts/, and write the three outputs to output/ as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
